hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing training regularization (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant models to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will produce a monotonic increase in cross-architecture representational alignment that saturates at a level indistinguishable from fully equivariant models, with the saturation point occurring at lower regularization strength for larger, more data-diverse training sets—a relationship that can be tested by training a series of Orb-style models with varying equigrad coefficients across multiple dataset scales and measuring their CKNNA alignment with MACE-class models.
